expert.definition: Inclined to lay down principles as incontrovertibly true.
expert.sentence: Her dogmatic insistence on a single approach stifled creative thinking.

expert.definition: Cheerful and full of energy.
expert.sentence: The ebullient crowd cheered from the moment the gates opened.

expert.definition: Outstandingly bad; shockingly obvious.
expert.sentence: The egregious error in the final paragraph had to be corrected immediately.

expert.definition: A speech or piece of writing that praises someone highly.
expert.sentence: The retiring head teacher was given a heartfelt encomium by the governors.

expert.definition: Difficult to interpret or understand; mysterious.
expert.sentence: The artist's enigmatic smile appeared in many of the self-portraits.

expert.definition: To use ambiguous language to conceal the truth or avoid commitment.
expert.sentence: The politician seemed to equivocate rather than answer directly.

expert.definition: Having or showing great knowledge or learning.
expert.sentence: The erudite professor could hold a conversation on almost any subject.

expert.definition: Intended for or understood by only a small group with specialised knowledge.
expert.sentence: The lecture touched on several esoteric branches of mathematics.

expert.definition: Extremely delicate and light; heavenly and spiritual.
expert.sentence: The music had an ethereal quality that seemed to fill the whole building.

expert.definition: A mild or indirect word used in place of one that might seem harsh.
expert.sentence: Passed away is a common euphemism for died.

expert.definition: Done with speed and efficiency.
expert.sentence: She made an expeditious decision and acted on it at once.

expert.definition: Very attentive to detail; having high standards of cleanliness.
expert.sentence: She was fastidious about keeping her notes neat and well organised.

expert.definition: Not showing a serious or respectful attitude.
expert.sentence: His flippant response to the serious question surprised the panel.

expert.definition: Happening by chance rather than by design; lucky.
expert.sentence: The fortuitous meeting changed the direction of her entire career.

expert.definition: Excessively talkative, especially on trivial matters.
expert.sentence: The garrulous market trader talked to every customer at great length.

expert.definition: Done without good reason; uncalled for.
expert.sentence: The gratuitous comments added nothing useful to the discussion.

expert.definition: A person or thing that announces or signals the approach of something.
expert.sentence: The first snowdrops are a harbinger of spring.

expert.definition: Leadership or dominance, especially of one country over others.
expert.sentence: The essay discussed the historical hegemony of certain trading nations.

expert.definition: A person who attacks widely accepted beliefs or traditions.
expert.sentence: The young designer was an iconoclast who rejected every conventional rule.

expert.definition: A distinctive habit or characteristic peculiar to an individual.
expert.sentence: Collecting antique maps was just one of his many examples of idiosyncrasy.

expert.definition: Deserving or causing public disgrace or shame.
expert.sentence: The team suffered an ignominious defeat in the very first round.

expert.definition: Acting or done quickly without thought or care.
expert.sentence: Her impetuous decision to change everything at the last minute caused chaos.

expert.definition: Not resulting from deliberate planning; accidental.
expert.sentence: The inadvertent mistake in the programme was quickly corrected.

expert.definition: Emitting light as a result of great heat; extremely angry.
expert.sentence: The incandescent response of the head teacher surprised everyone present.

expert.definition: Not in harmony with the surroundings; out of place.
expert.sentence: The modern sculpture looked incongruous beside the ancient church.

expert.definition: Wanting to avoid activity; habitually lazy.
expert.sentence: The indolent student rarely made it to morning lessons on time.

expert.definition: Impossible to stop or prevent; relentless.
expert.sentence: The inexorable advance of technology changed the way we all communicate.

expert.definition: Clever, original, and inventive.
expert.sentence: She came up with an ingenious solution to the engineering problem.

expert.definition: Inborn; natural rather than acquired.
expert.sentence: She had an innate ability to put people at ease.

expert.definition: Lacking flavour; lacking strong qualities of any kind.
expert.sentence: The insipid performance did nothing to excite the audience.

expert.definition: Unwilling to change one's views or to agree.
expert.sentence: The intransigent committee refused to consider any alternative plan.

expert.definition: Having a habit or activity so firmly established that it is unlikely to change.
expert.sentence: She was an inveterate reader who always had a book in her bag.

expert.definition: Having or showing a tendency to be easily angered.
expert.sentence: The irascible old professor was known for his fierce but fair criticism.

expert.definition: Fond of joking; humorous.
expert.sentence: He was in a jocular mood and kept the whole table laughing.

expert.definition: Using very few words to express a great deal.
expert.sentence: His laconic reply told her everything she needed to know.

expert.definition: A state of physical or mental tiredness and lack of energy.
expert.sentence: A feeling of lassitude overcame her on the long, hot afternoon.

expert.definition: The quality of talking a great deal; talkativeness.
expert.sentence: His loquacity made it almost impossible to get a word in edgeways.

expert.definition: Giving off light, especially in the dark; very bright.
expert.sentence: The luminous stars were reflected in the still lake below.

expert.definition: Using high-flown or bombastic language.
expert.sentence: His magniloquent speech impressed no one and exhausted most of the audience.

expert.definition: Having or showing a wish to do evil to others.
expert.sentence: The villain's malevolent grin made the audience shudder.

expert.definition: Apparently attractive but having in reality no value or integrity.
expert.sentence: The meretricious packaging disguised the poor quality of the contents.

expert.definition: A person who dislikes and distrusts other people generally.
expert.sentence: Despite his reputation as a misanthrope, he was generous to those he trusted.

expert.definition: Larger or more generous than is usual or necessary.
expert.sentence: The munificent donation paid for an entire new wing of the library.

expert.definition: In the form of a cloud; unclear, hazy, or indistinct.
expert.sentence: Her ideas for the project remained nebulous for several weeks.

expert.definition: Stubbornly refusing to change one's position, especially in the face of argument.
expert.sentence: She remained obdurate despite all the evidence presented against her view.

expert.definition: Not direct in expression; indirect; not straight.
expert.sentence: He made an oblique reference to the problem without naming it directly.

expert.definition: Asserting authority in a domineering way; intrusively helpful.
expert.sentence: The officious attendant insisted on checking every ticket twice.

expert.definition: Knowing everything; having complete or unlimited knowledge.
expert.sentence: The narrator of the novel was omniscient, aware of every character's thoughts.

expert.definition: Richly luxurious; ostentatiously wealthy.
expert.sentence: The opulent interior of the palace dazzled every visitor.

expert.definition: A typical example or pattern of something; a model.
expert.sentence: The experiment was described as a paradigm of careful scientific method.

expert.definition: Having a harmful effect, especially in a gradual or subtle way.
expert.sentence: The pernicious influence of rumours spread quickly through the school.

expert.definition: The quality of having a ready insight into things; shrewdness.
expert.sentence: Her perspicacity in spotting the flaw impressed everyone on the panel.

expert.definition: Childishly sulky or bad-tempered.
expert.sentence: The petulant response did nothing to help resolve the situation.

expert.definition: A person who seeks to promote the welfare of others, especially through generous donations.
expert.sentence: The philanthropist funded the new science block at the local school.

expert.definition: A diplomat with full power to act independently.
expert.sentence: The plenipotentiary arrived to negotiate the terms of the agreement.

expert.definition: Of great significance; done with a pompous air of great importance.
expert.sentence: The portentous silence before the announcement was almost unbearable.

expert.definition: Dealing with things sensibly and practically, rather than theoretically.
expert.sentence: She took a pragmatic approach and focused on what could be done.

expert.definition: Dangerously high or steep; done suddenly and without careful consideration.
expert.sentence: The precipitous drop on the far side of the ridge was alarming.

expert.definition: Failing to observe the limits of what is permitted; overconfident.
expert.sentence: It was presumptuous of him to assume he had won before the results were read.

expert.definition: Recklessly extravagant; licentious; dissolute.
expert.sentence: His profligate spending left him with nothing saved by the end of term.

expert.definition: Giving or indicating a good chance of success; favourable.
expert.sentence: The calm weather was a propitious sign for the outdoor event.

expert.definition: Childishly silly and trivial.
expert.sentence: The puerile behaviour in the corridor was completely unacceptable.

expert.definition: Eager or quick to argue, quarrel, or fight.
expert.sentence: The pugnacious debater challenged every point raised by the other side.

expert.definition: Formidable, especially as an opponent.
expert.sentence: The redoubtable champion had never lost a single match in three years.

expert.definition: Optimistic or positive, especially in a difficult situation.
expert.sentence: She remained sanguine about the chances of success despite the obstacles.

expert.definition: Relating to the idea that only one's own mind is real or knowable.
expert.sentence: His solipsistic worldview meant he rarely considered anyone else's perspective.

expert.definition: Having a deep, full, rich sound.
expert.sentence: The sonorous voice of the narrator filled the entire auditorium.

expert.definition: Not being what it purports to be; false or fake.
expert.sentence: The examiner dismissed the spurious claim without further consideration.

expert.definition: A person who behaves in an ingratiating way to gain advantage.
expert.sentence: The sycophant agreed with every word the manager said.

expert.definition: Tending to keep a firm hold of something; persistent.
expert.sentence: Her tenacious spirit carried her through the most difficult periods.

expert.definition: Easily frightened; showing a great lack of courage.
expert.sentence: The timorous character retreated at the very first sign of difficulty.

expert.definition: Easy to deal with; docile and manageable.
expert.sentence: The tractable nature of the material made it easy to shape.

expert.definition: Eager or quick to argue or fight; aggressively defiant.
expert.sentence: The truculent response shocked everyone who had expected a calm discussion.

expert.definition: Swollen and distended; pompous or bombastic in style.
expert.sentence: The turgid prose was almost impossible to wade through.

expert.definition: The fact of appearing or being found everywhere.
expert.sentence: The ubiquity of mobile phones has changed how people communicate.

expert.definition: Lacking meaning, substance, or intelligence.
expert.sentence: The vacuous response failed to address a single point that had been raised.

expert.definition: Showing or motivated by susceptibility to bribery.
expert.sentence: The venal official was eventually dismissed for accepting bribes.

expert.definition: Using more words than needed; wordy.
expert.sentence: The verbose essay was halved in length without losing any important meaning.

expert.definition: Causing annoyance, frustration, or worry.
expert.sentence: The vexatious delays made the project almost impossible to complete on time.

expert.definition: Experienced through another person rather than directly.
expert.sentence: She gained vicarious pleasure from reading about others' adventures.

expert.definition: Having or showing a strong desire for revenge.
expert.sentence: The vindictive character spent years plotting against those who had wronged him.

expert.definition: Filled with bitter criticism or malice.
expert.sentence: The vitriolic review was so harsh that many readers felt it unfair.

expert.definition: Talking fluently, readily, and at length.
expert.sentence: The voluble guide described every exhibit in extraordinary detail.

expert.definition: Wanting or devouring great quantities; very eager.
expert.sentence: She was a voracious reader who finished a new book every few days.

expert.definition: Having or showing a feeling of vague or regretful longing.
expert.sentence: She gave a wistful smile when she thought about her old school.

expert.definition: Fanaticism; the character or behaviour of a zealot.
expert.sentence: The zealotry of his approach made it impossible to have a calm debate.

expert.definition: A state of uncertainty about what to do in a difficult situation.
expert.sentence: She was in a quandary about which option to choose.

expert.definition: Deceit used to achieve a goal; a trick or stratagem.
expert.sentence: He resorted to subterfuge to avoid the difficult question.

expert.definition: Carried out without real care, effort, or thought; routine.
expert.sentence: She gave only a perfunctory glance at the instructions.

expert.definition: To find a way around an obstacle or rule.
expert.sentence: She tried to circumvent the problem rather than face it head-on.

expert.definition: A collection of concise information on a particular subject.
expert.sentence: He used a compendium of natural history facts for his project.

expert.definition: So unusual or skilled as to be impossible to copy.
expert.sentence: The comedian had an inimitable style that no one could replicate.

expert.definition: Excessively unwilling to spend money; extremely frugal.
expert.sentence: The parsimonious landlord refused to fix the broken heating.

expert.definition: Clearly expressed and easy to understand.
expert.sentence: Her perspicuous explanation helped even the most confused pupils.
